title: Gastronema helictum
taxonomy: Gastronema helictum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to Australia and is found in the eastern and northern regions. Gastronema helictum is a type of climber or shrub that produces white or pink flowers. Its classification also includes the genus name Gastronema, which is part of the larger classification of plants. Further classification details include the family Apocynaceae and the order Gentianales.
uses: Gastronema helictum has been traditionally used in various medicinal practices due to its potential health benefits. Its leaves and stems are often employed in the preparation of herbal remedies, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Research suggests that the plant may help alleviate symptoms associated with fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Gastronema helictum is also used in traditional medicine to treat various gastrointestinal issues and as an antidote for certain poisonous substances. However, further scientific studies are necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

intro: Gastronema helictum, commonly known as Indian spurge or wild cabbage, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. Traditionally, Gastronema helictum has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gastronema helictum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, Gastronema helictum is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, and is used to make herbal preparations such as churna (powder) and kwath (decoction) to treat various health conditions.
